commit.message:
Skip magic-nix-cache-action on darwin to fix 22-minute post-run (#54)

* Initial plan

* fix: skip magic-nix-cache-action on darwin to prevent 22-minute post-run

The Post Run magic-nix-cache-action step was taking 22+ minutes on the
darwin (aaron) build job. The action uploads all newly built Nix store
paths to GitHub Actions Cache one-by-one (~1-2s each). On aarch64-darwin
many packages aren't pre-cached, resulting in hundreds of uploads.

Since cachix-action already handles persistent caching for darwin in ~10s,
skip magic-nix-cache-action for the darwin platform entirely.

commit.message:
fix(admin): use import.meta.glob for locale catalog resolution (#499)

* fix(admin): use import.meta.glob for locale catalog resolution

The dynamic import in admin.astro used a template literal to load locale
catalogs cross-package, which failed in non-monorepo installs because
Vite's SSR module runner couldn't resolve the path. The Vite alias entry
intended to fix this used `*` in the find string, which is treated as a
literal character, not a wildcard.

Moves locale loading into the admin package via `loadMessages()` using
`import.meta.glob`, so Vite resolves catalogs at build time with
reliable relative paths. Removes the no-op Vite alias.

* fix(locales): use Messages type from @lingui/core and DEFAULT_LOCALE for fallback

commit.message:
Copy-on-write SD storage — no more torn blocks

All SD writes now allocate a fresh slot, write data, then swap
the SRAM index pointer and free the old slot. Power loss mid-write
leaves old data intact.

Changes:
- Parallel g_slots[] array tracks SD slot per key (COW lookup)
- KVSD_INDEX_MAX halved 36K→18K (key+slot = same 144KB footprint)
- find_slot_for_key uses SRAM index (O(log n), no SD I/O)
- kvsd_put always COW: alloc new → write → swap → free old
- Keylist format: 8 bytes per entry (key+slot) vs old 4 bytes
- Bitmap scan fallback reads key footer from each card block
- Requires SD wipe before first use (format change)

Benchmarks (clean SD):
- Seq writes: 27/sec (vs 31 without COW — extra bitmap I/O)
- Mixed 30s: 30.9 ops/sec, 928 total, 21% errors
- All reads/queries unaffected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent: Project Organization Sweep (#8389)

* Agent: Project Organization Sweep

* Move isIdentifier from lint/rule-helpers.ts to core/utils/regexp.ts as isGmlIdentifierName

Original location: src/lint/src/rules/gml/rule-helpers.ts (isIdentifier)
New location: src/core/src/utils/regexp.ts (isGmlIdentifierName)

Rationale: The function tests whether a string matches the GML identifier
pattern, which is already canonically defined in core as
GML_IDENTIFIER_NAME_PATTERN. Placing the convenience wrapper alongside
its pattern in core eliminates duplication and makes the function
available to all workspaces (refactor, CLI, lint transforms already use
the raw pattern). The function was previously co-located with ESLint
context helpers (readObjectOption, shouldReportUnsafe) that have no
domain relationship with GML identifier validation.

commit.message:
fix(jetson): replace header-based RLS with RPC functions for device sync

Root cause: Supabase gateway does not forward custom X-Device-Token
headers to PostgREST, making header-based RLS policies ineffective.
All device queries (config poll, config ACK, heartbeat, prediction
push) returned empty results, causing ONLINE=0 and Config=Pending.

Solution: Created SECURITY DEFINER RPC functions that accept
device_token as a parameter (same pattern as provision_device RPC):
- device_poll_config(token) - returns desired_config, status, user_id
- device_ack_config(token, config) - ACK applied config
- device_heartbeat(token) - update last_seen_at + status
- device_push_predictions(token, predictions) - batch insert with
  device ownership validation

Files changed:
- NEW: database/migrations/020_device_sync_rpcs.sql
- jetson/app/sync_engine.py: all 4 operations now use RPC POST
- docs/guides/jetson_deployment_guide.md: updated sync section
- docs/guides/supabase_setup.md: added migrations 018-020
- docs/technical/project_context.md: updated directory tree

commit.message:
Optimize OCR pipeline: compact format, parallelism, tests (#2)

* Optimize OCR pipeline: compact format, parallelism, tests

Changes to ocr_roerich.py:
- Switch from JSON to compact line-oriented format (T:/W:/E:/R:/S: + ===)
  for model output - saves ~52% output tokens
- Add parse_compact_entries() to convert compact format to JSON for storage
- Add ThreadPoolExecutor with --workers flag (default 20) for parallel OCR
- Add ProgressTracker with thread-safe velocity, ETA, progress bar
- Add --test flag to write output to roerich_test_output/ instead of
  production dir, keeping existing results safe
- Fix error file handling: use 'raw is not None' instead of 'raw in dir()',
  include error message in .error files, clean up stale .error files on
  successful re-processing
- Pre-extract all page images before parallel OCR phase
- Prompt improvements: explicit 'keep fields on single line' instruction

New file test_parse_compact.py:
- 26 unit tests covering basic parsing, page boundary flags, markdown
  fence stripping, edge cases, and real Gemini output samples

Tested: 2 pages with --test flag, 0 errors, valid JSON output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix NoneType crash: handle empty Gemini responses with retry

Gemini API can return response.text=None (safety filters, rate limits,
transient errors). This caused 'NoneType has no attribute strip' crashes.

Fixes:
- ocr_page_gemini() now retries up to 3 times with backoff (5/15/30s)
  on empty responses and transient API errors
- Logs block reason / finish_reason when available for diagnostics
- parse_compact_entries() guards against None input as safety net
- Added test for None input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Retry only on 5xx server errors, not empty responses

Empty responses mean the page has no text - retrying won't help.
Only retry once on clear 5xx errors (500/502/503/504).

Hide delete button on dashboard when all players have voted (≥4 players) (#36)

Prevents accidental deletion of complete games with meaningful
participation by hiding the "🗑️ Delete game" button when every voter has
rated all songs and there are at least 4 voters.

- **`dashboard.tsx`**: Fetches voters and votes, computes
`hideDeleteButton` via a vote-count Map (O(n+m)), passes it to
`GameControls` in both open and closed game paths
- **`game-controls.tsx`**: Accepts optional `hideDeleteButton` prop,
conditionally renders the delete button

```tsx
const voteCountByVoter = new Map<string, number>();
for (const v of votes) {
  voteCountByVoter.set(v.voter, (voteCountByVoter.get(v.voter) ?? 0) + 1);
}
const hideDeleteButton =
  voters.length >= 4 &&
  totalSongs > 0 &&
  voters.every(
    (voter) => (voteCountByVoter.get(voter.name) ?? 0) >= totalSongs,
  );
```

commit.message:
fix: track cumulative offset in memory across days (race condition)

Critical fix: async_add_external_statistics does NOT immediately commit
to the recorder DB. When processing multiple days in one update cycle,
reading the offset from DB for each day returned stale values, causing
all days to have overlapping cumulative sums.

Now follows enelgrid's pattern exactly:
1. Read offset ONCE from DB before the loop
2. Pass offset as parameter to _import_15min_statistics
3. Return updated offset for chaining to next day
4. Log at INFO level for easier debugging

Also changed _import_15min_statistics signature to accept and return
the cumulative offset instead of reading from DB internally.

repository.description: An evidence-based plugin for generating and optimizing AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md configuration files for AI coding agents. Instead of auto-generating one bloated file, this plugin creates minimal, scoped files following progressive disclosure principles.

feat(cache): Phase 6 distribution headers — IDistributedEviction, ICachePartition, IAdaptiveTTLPolicy (#4476)

* Initial plan

* feat(cache): implement Phase 6 distribution headers + update docs

- Add include/cache/distributed_eviction.h: IDistributedEviction interface
  for cross-node coordinated eviction (evict, evictByPattern, evictByTenant,
  flush, listener registration, DistributedEvictionEvent, DistributedEvictionStats)
- Add include/cache/cache_partition.h: ICachePartition interface for sharded
  per-tenant partition management (assignTenant, unassignTenant, listTenants,
  listPartitions, resize, evictPartition, getStats, getAllStats, PartitionStats)
- Add include/cache/adaptive_ttl_policy.h: IAdaptiveTTLPolicy interface for
  stateful history-driven TTL adaptation (recordAccess, computeTTL, getHistory,
  evict, pruneHistory, flushHistory, configure, AdaptiveTTLSuggestion, AccessRecord,
  AdaptiveTTLPolicyConfig)
- Add tests/test_cache_phase6_interfaces.cpp with 37 unit tests covering all
  three interfaces via mock concrete implementations
- Register CachePhase6InterfacesTests in tests/CMakeLists.txt
- Update include/cache/ROADMAP.md: Phase 6 items marked [x], status bumped to v2.0.0
- Update include/cache/FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md: all planned features marked [x],
  design constraints marked [x], Phase 6 section added
- Update src/cache/FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md: SLO Monitor Latency Percentile items
  marked [x]; design constraints marked [x]

commit.message:
rpc: fix CValidationState incomplete type in csapp RPCs

CommitTransaction's CValidationState* parameter is optional (defaults to
nullptr). Remove the explicit CValidationState local variable — its full
definition lives in consensus/validation.h which is not pulled in by the
rpc/csapp.cpp include chain, causing 'incomplete type' build errors on CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
